sections.paragraph:
συλλαλέω impf. συνελάλουν; 1 aor. συνελάλησα (Polyb.; OGI 229, 23; pap., LXX) to exchange thoughts with, talk/discuss/converse with τινί someone (Polyb. 4, 22, 8; PHib 66, 4 [III b.c.]; PRainer 18, 23; Ex 34:35; Pr 6:22; Is 7:6; TestJob 30:3 ἀλλήλοις) Mk 9:4; Lk 9:30; 22:4. Also μετά τινος Mt 17:3; Ac 18:12 D; 25:12. συνελάλουν πρὸς ἀλλήλους λέγοντες Lk 4:36.—M-M s.v. συνλαλέω.

impf. impf. = imperfect

aor. aor. = aorist

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

PHib PHib = The Hibeh Papyri I–II—List 4

PRainer PRainer = Papyrus Rainer (Archduke of Austria), s. CPR—Lists 4

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 955
